qa_pairs.question: Why is it difficult for CCDB to determine which site a sponsored user has access to?
qa_pairs.answer: It is very difficult for CCDB to know what site a sponsored user has access to at any given time because it requires looking at 1) the tier service of which their PI is a member, 2) what system the sponsored user requested access (if they requested access at all) and 3) the `aip-` RAP that the sponsored user is a member of (if this is actually the case).

qa_pairs.question: Where can PAICE sites reliably get a list of usernames for active site users to send communications?
qa_pairs.answer: If a PAICE site needs to get a list of usernames to send communications to users that are actively using their site, they cannot reliably get this from CCDB and must then get it from LDAP or by looking at their own systems.

qa_pairs.question: What types of roles does this page focus on when retrieving access lists?
qa_pairs.answer: This page focuses on how to get the list of PI roles (but not sponsored roles) that have requested Tier access and system access on any PAICE site.

qa_pairs.question: What permissions are required to get the list of PI roles with Tier and system access on a PAICE site?
qa_pairs.answer: Any administrator with a Privileged Federation staff role or higher permissions on CCDB can get the list of PI roles that have requested Tier access and system access on any PAICE site.

qa_pairs.question: What is the naming convention for Tier 1 and Tier 2 services?
qa_pairs.answer: Tier 1 and Tier 2 have services that follow the convention `aip-[site]-tier1`.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it not possible to identify who is in a particular system by only looking at Tier 3 or Tier 4 service members?
qa_pairs.answer: Tiers 3 and 4 have generic `aip-tier3` and `aip-[site]-tier1` services, which means that it is not possible who is in a particular system just by looking at the members of a Tier 3-4 service.

qa_pairs.question: What steps are required to determine which PI roles from a specific site have requested Tier 3 and/or Tier 4 access?
qa_pairs.answer: To see which PI roles from a particular site (for example, Mila) have requested Tier 3 and/or Tier 4 access, there are two lists that need to be downloaded and some crosscheck needs to be done between the two.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to get a list of users with Tier access and system access?
qa_pairs.answer: Go to `Browse` → `Services` in the main CCDB menu.

qa_pairs.question: How do you find generic Tier 3 services within the CCDB Services menu?
qa_pairs.answer: Type in “tier3” in the search box of the Generic services table.

qa_pairs.question: Which link should be clicked to access the `aip-tier3` service page?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on `aip-tier3` under the “Name” column.

qa_pairs.question: How do you export the list of roles that have requested Tier 3 service access?
qa_pairs.answer: Once inside the `aip-tier3` service page, click on the '''Export to CSV''' button.

qa_pairs.question: What information does the CSV exported from the `aip-tier3` service page contain?
qa_pairs.answer: This CSV will provide the list of any active role that has requested access to a Tier3 service (but this list does not specifically show who has also requested access to the Tamia system).

qa_pairs.question: After exporting the Tier 3 service list, what is the next navigation step in CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: Go to the main Services page again (`Browse` → `Services` in the main CCDB menu).

qa_pairs.question: How do you find the Tamia system agreements on the Services page?
qa_pairs.answer: Scroll down to the bottom of the page and in the search box of the System agreements panel, type in “tamia”.

qa_pairs.question: Which link should be clicked to access the Tamia system access page?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on the `tamia-access` link under the “Name” column.

qa_pairs.question: How do you export the list of roles that have requested Tamia system access?
qa_pairs.answer: Once inside the tamia-access service page, click on the '''Export to CSV''' button.

qa_pairs.question: What information does the CSV exported from the `tamia-access` service page provide?
qa_pairs.answer: This CSV will provide the list of any active role that has requested access to the Tamia system (but it does not indicate which service, if any, the role is a member of).

qa_pairs.question: How can the two exported CSV lists (Tier 3 access and Tamia access) be combined to identify users with both accesses?
qa_pairs.answer: You can now put these two lists in Excel or Google Sheet and use a simple VLOOKUP formula cross check which users show in both: that is the list of users that requested Tier 3 access AND requested access to the Tamia system.

qa_pairs.question: How can this process be adapted to find users with Tier 1 or Tier 2 service access and Tamia access?
qa_pairs.answer: To get the list of users that requested access to a tier 1-2 service and to Tamia, follow the same process but simply replace `aip-tier3` with the appropriate `aip-[site]-tier1` or `aip-[site]-tier2`—the rest of the process should be identical.

qa_pairs.question: What should I expect if some users only have Tier 3 access but not Tamia access (or vice versa) when performing a VLOOKUP?
qa_pairs.answer: Note that some users may request access to a Tier 3 service but NOT to the Tamia system (or vice versa), so do not be surprised when you see that your VLOOKUP formula returns some ''#N/A''.
